title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign poses a significant threat to cloud-based infrastructure, potentially leading to data encryption, service disruptions, financial losses, and reputational damage. The use of compromised CI/CD pipelines increases the risk of widespread and rapid deployment of the ransomware.
recommended_actions: Immediately review and secure CI/CD pipelines by implementing strict access controls, multi-factor authentication, and regular security audits.
Deploy endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions to monitor for anomalous activity and ransomware indicators in cloud environments.
Ensure regular backups of critical data are performed and stored offline to enable recovery in the event of an attack.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: A vulnerability was determined in FoundationAgents MetaGPT up to 0.8.2. Affected by this issue is...
description: A vulnerability was determined in FoundationAgents MetaGPT up to 0.8.2. Affected by this issue is the function check_cmd_exists of the file metagpt/utils/common.py. This manipulation of the argument mermaid.path causes command injection. The attack may be initiated remotely. A high degree of complexity is needed for the attack. The exploitation is known to be difficult. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue rep

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote command injection via the mermaid.path argument in the check_cmd_exists function, potentially enabling attackers to execute arbitrary commands. While exploitation is complex and difficult, the public disclosure increases the likelihood of targeted attacks.
recommended_actions: Update FoundationAgents MetaGPT to the latest patched version once available.
Restrict access to systems running vulnerable versions and monitor for unusual activity.
Validate and sanitize all user inputs to prevent command injection vulnerabilities.

description: A flaw has been found in GL.iNet GL-MT3000 4.4.5. This impacts the function snprintf of the file /cgi-bin/glc of the component FTP Protocol Handler. Executing a manipulation of the argument media_dir can lead to command injection. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. Upgrading to version 4.8.1 will fix this issue. ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in GL.iNet GL-MT3000 4.4.5 allows for remote command injection via the FTP Protocol Handler. If exploited, attackers could execute arbitrary commands, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or system compromise.
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade to GL.iNet GL-MT3000 firmware version 4.8.1 to patch the vulnerability.
Restrict FTP access to trusted IP addresses and disable FTP services if not required.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and implement intrusion detection systems to identify potential exploitation attempts.

title: A vulnerability was identified in theonedev onedev up to 15.0.5. This vulnerability affects the...
description: A vulnerability was identified in theonedev onedev up to 15.0.5. This vulnerability affects the function canAccessIssue of the file /issues/ of the component Pull Request Handler. It is advisable to upgrade the affected component.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Pull Request Handler's authorization mechanism could allow unauthorized access to sensitive issue data, leading to potential data breaches or unauthorized modifications.
recommended_actions: Upgrade theonedev to version 15.0.6 immediately to patch the vulnerability.
Review access logs for any unauthorized access to issues and pull requests to identify potential exploitation.
Implement additional monitoring and authorization checks for sensitive components like issue tracking and pull request handling.

title: A vulnerability was detected in Mage AI up to 0.9.79. This impacts the function useMutation of...
description: A vulnerability was detected in Mage AI up to 0.9.79. This impacts the function useMutation of the file mage_ai/frontend/components/Sessions/SignForm/index.tsx of the component Sign-in Flow. Performing a manipulation of the argument query.redirect_url results in cross site scripting. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit is now public and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Mage AI up to version 0.9.79 allows for cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks via the manipulation of the 'query.redirect_url' argument in the Sign-in Flow. This could enable attackers to execute malicious scripts in the context of a user's browser, potentially leading to data theft, session hijacking, or unauthorized actions on behalf of the user.
recommended_actions: Immediately update Mage AI to a patched version if available, or implement input validation and sanitization for the 'query.redirect_url' parameter to mitigate the vulnerability.
Monitor application logs and traffic for signs of exploitation, such as unusual redirects or unauthorized script execution.
Notify users and administrators of the vulnerability, and provide guidance on recognizing and avoiding potential phishing attempts or malicious links.

title: CVE-2026-10167 - A weakness has been identified in OUSL-GROUP-BrinaryBrains School Student Management System up to 1e
description: A weakness has been identified in OUSL-GROUP-BrinaryBrains School Student Management System up to 1e70e5ad1125b86dca4ee086eb6bb121f17708b6. This impacts the function sign_auth_cookie of the file application/controllers/Login.php of the component MY_Controller. Executing a manipulation of the argument role can lead to improper authentication. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. This product takes the approach of rolling releases to provide continious delivery. Therefore, version details for affected and updated releases are not available. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

title: CVE-2026-8382 - The Advanced Custom Fields (ACF®) plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authorization bypass in all
description: The Advanced Custom Fields (ACF®) plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authorization bypass in all versions up to, and including, 6.8.1. This is due to the plugin not properly verifying that a user is authorized to perform an action. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to overwrite the post_title and post_content of any post bound to a publicly accessible acf_form() instance by injecting values into the _post_title and _post_content parameters of a form submission request.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to bypass authorization checks and modify the title and content of posts linked to publicly accessible ACF forms. This could lead to defacement, misinformation, or malicious content injection, potentially damaging the credibility and trust of affected websites.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the Advanced Custom Fields (ACF®) plugin to a patched version (if available) or disable the plugin temporarily if no fix is available.
Audit all publicly accessible ACF forms and restrict access to trusted users only by implementing additional authentication and authorization mechanisms.
Monitor website content for unauthorized changes and implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious form submissions.
